evals.query: Scaffold a contract-first Kora HTTP server from an OpenAPI spec with GET /users/{userId}. Wire the Gradle generation and implement the delegate so it returns the user or 404.
expected_behavior: Applies the org.openapi.generator plugin version 7.14.0 and puts ru.tinkoff.kora:openapi-generator on the buildscript classpath
Registers a GenerateTask with generatorName = "kora", configOptions mode = java-server, and a unique outputDir wired into sourceSets and compileJava.dependsOn
Adds the Java annotation processor ru.tinkoff.kora:annotation-processors and the ru.tinkoff.kora:http-server-undertow module via the kora-parent BOM
Implements UsersApiDelegate annotated with @Component returning GetUser200ApiResponse or GetUser404ApiResponse
Does not return ResponseEntity, does not use field injection, and does not hand-write @HttpController/@HttpRoute for the generated API

evals.query: Add server-side validation to a Kora OpenAPI POST /users endpoint: name 1-100 chars, email required. Map validation failures to HTTP 400.
expected_behavior: Sets enableServerValidation = true (and enableServerValidationInterceptor = true) in configOptions
Adds the ru.tinkoff.kora:validation-module dependency and ValidationModule to the @KoraApp interface
Notes that generated DTOs carry Kora validation annotations from ru.tinkoff.kora.validation.common.annotation (e.g. @Size, @NotNull)
Provides a ViolationExceptionHttpServerResponseMapper to return 400
Does not use Jakarta Bean Validation or Hibernate Validator annotations

evals.query: My OpenAPI schema uses oneOf + allOf with a discriminator but the generator collapses it into one type. How do I generate the polymorphic types correctly in Kora?
expected_behavior: Sets openapiNormalizer = [DISABLE_ALL: "true"] to keep oneOf/allOf intact
Explains that since plugin 7.0.0 the SIMPLIFY_ONEOF_ANYOF rule is enabled by default and rewrites polymorphic schemas
Keeps a discriminator propertyName with an explicit mapping in the contract
Shows the delegate checking the discriminator value before handling each variant
Does not invent generator options and does not rely on reflection-based polymorphism

evals.query: My OpenAPI contract declares a bearerAuth securityScheme. How do I make the Kora-generated server authenticate requests?
expected_behavior: Provides an HttpServerPrincipalExtractor tagged with @Tag(ApiSecurity.BearerAuth.class)
Imports the generated ApiSecurity class from the configured apiPackage, not from a Kora framework package
Returns a CompletableFuture of a type implementing ru.tinkoff.kora.common.Principal (PrincipalWithScopes when scopes are needed)
Notes the tag class name follows the scheme name (oAuth maps to ApiSecurity.OAuth, not OAuth2)
Does not add auth annotations to the delegate and does not introduce a non-Kora security framework

evals.query: Generate a Kotlin Kora OpenAPI server using coroutines and implement the suspend delegate for a user lookup.
expected_behavior: Sets configOptions mode = kotlin-suspend-server
Adds the KSP plugin and ksp("ru.tinkoff.kora:symbol-processors") with the kora-parent BOM
Implements the delegate as a suspend function returning the sealed *ApiResponses type, annotated with @Component
Wires generation into the Kotlin source set and orders the KspTask after the GenerateTask
Does not use ksp("ru.tinkoff.kora:kora-ksp") and does not block inside the suspend delegate

evals.query: What server modes does the Kora OpenAPI generator support and how does the delegate return type change?
expected_behavior: Lists java-server, java-async-server, java-reactive-server, kotlin-server, kotlin-suspend-server
Explains java-server returns the sealed *ApiResponses record, java-async-server returns CompletionStage, java-reactive-server returns Mono
Notes java-reactive-server requires adding io.projectreactor:reactor-core
States the delegate always returns the generated sealed response, never ResponseEntity

evals.query: Expose the OpenAPI document and a Swagger UI from my Kora OpenAPI server.
expected_behavior: Adds the ru.tinkoff.kora:openapi-management dependency and OpenApiManagementModule to the @KoraApp interface
Enables openapi.management with endpoint /openapi and swaggerui endpoint /swagger-ui in config
Keeps publicApiHttpPort for traffic separate from privateApiHttpPort for probes/metrics
Does not pull in a non-Kora OpenAPI documentation library

evals.query: I need two OpenAPI contracts generated into one Kora application. How do I configure both without conflicts?
expected_behavior: Registers a separate GenerateTask per contract, each with a unique outputDir
Gives each task its own apiPackage/modelPackage and wires both into sourceSets
Adds compileJava.dependsOn (Java) or KspTask dependsOn (Kotlin) for each task
Implements one @Component delegate per generated *ApiDelegate
Does not reuse a single outputDir for both tasks and does not edit files under build/generated
